headline: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
summary: Britain on Friday designated ‚Äåcloud service providers Microsoft, ‚ÄåGoogle, Amazon and Oracle as 'critical ‚Äãthird parties', bringing them under direct regulatory oversight to protect the country's financial ‚Äåsystem. "As banks, ‚ÅÝinsurers and financial market infrastructures become increasingly ‚ÅÝreliant on cloud services, disruption at a major ‚Äãsupplier could ‚Äãaffect ‚Äãmultiple firms at ‚Äåthe same time, potentially impacting services customers depend on," the government said in a statement. To that end, ‚Äåit designated Microsoft ‚ÄãIreland Operations ‚ÄãLtd, Google ‚ÄãCloud EMEA Ltd, ‚ÄåAmazon Web Services ‚ÄãEMEA SARL, ‚Äãand Oracle Corporation UK Ltd as critical third ‚Äãparties, ‚Äåeffective July 13.

headline: Energy and Utilities Analytics Market Report Now Available; Profiles IBM, Siemens, ABB, GE Vernova, Microsoft & 34 Others; Forecasts Market Growth from $6.1B (2026) to $10.1B by 2031
summary: The energy and utilities analytics market is set for robust growth, increasing from over USD 6.10 billion in 2026 to over USD 10.10 billion by 2031, signifying a CAGR of 10.6%. Key growth drivers include grid modernization, renewable energy integration, and smart meter rollouts. The market faces challenges such as legacy infrastructure and cybersecurity concerns. Opportunities exist in AI-driven maintenance and edge analytics. The cloud-based deployment mode and Asia Pacific region are projected
